question_text: Which statement best describes what happens to gas pressure when the temperature of a gas increases in a sealed container?

options.label: A
options.text: Pressure decreases because hot particles move slowly

options.label: B
options.text: Pressure increases because hotter particles move faster and collide more forcefully

options.label: C
options.text: Pressure stays the same because the number of particles doesn't change

options.label: D
options.text: Pressure increases because new particles are created

explanation: When gas is heated, the particles move faster and collide harder and more frequently with the container walls. This increases the pressure even though the volume and number of particles stay the same.
